item.title: 8 Incident Response Plan Mistakes That Lead to Failures
item.description:
Learn the 8 critical incident response mistakes that leave 73% of organizations unprepared, and how to close the execution gaps that turn manageable cyber incidents into full-scale business crises.

The post 8 Incident Response Plan Mistakes That Lead to Failures appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Sygnia Appoints Ran Buck as Chief Revenue Officer to Accelerate Global Growth
item.description:
Cyber readiness and response leader expands go-to-market leadership as organizations seek trusted partners across the full lifecycle of cyber incidents

The post Sygnia Appoints Ran Buck as Chief Revenue Officer to Accelerate Global Growth appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Incident Response Metrics That Actually Matter to Boards (And the Ones That Don’t)
item.description:
Learn which incident response metrics boards actually need to make decisions, and which ones create false confidence.

item.title: The Anatomy of Cyber Attacks Affecting OT Organizations
item.description:
Join us for a practical session based on Sygnia’s field experience across dozens of industrial and OT-centric incidents.

The post The Anatomy of Cyber Attacks Affecting OT Organizations appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Velvet Ant’s Operation Highland: How a China-Nexus Actor Infiltrated an Internal Network Undetected
item.description:
Discover the detailed forensic investigation by Sygnia into the sophisticated cyber attack by Velvet Ant on a major organization.

The post Velvet Ant’s Operation Highland: How a China-Nexus Actor Infiltrated an Internal Network Undetected appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: 2026 Gartner® Hype Cycle™ for CPS Security
item.description:

item.title: The Crypto Threat Environment 2026
item.description:
Understanding how crypto breaches occur and what separates organizations that contain them from those that suffer catastrophic losses

The post The Crypto Threat Environment 2026 appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: 83% of Crypto Security Leaders Report Cyberattack Exposure as Sygnia Releases Bybit Investigation Findings
item.description:
Sygnia's investigation into the $1.5 billion Bybit hack reveals how North Korea's Lazarus Group exploited human risk, social engineering, and crypto infrastructure weaknesses to execute the largest crypto theft in history.

The post 83% of Crypto Security Leaders Report Cyberattack Exposure as Sygnia Releases Bybit Investigation Findings appeared first on Sygnia.

item.title: Crypto Security in 2026: Why Transaction Authority Is the Most Important Security Control
item.description:
Most crypto breaches occur when attackers reach transaction authority. Learn how to secure identities, infrastructure, and authorization systems.

The post Crypto Security in 2026: Why Transaction Authority Is the Most Important Security Control appeared first on Sygnia.
